Applicant Name: West Morgan-East Lawrence Water & Sewer Authority
Attachment Project Name:
EC Removal and Interconnection to
West Lawrence, Hartselle, and Falkville Water Systems
Attachment Project Description: The West-Morgan/East Lawrence Water Authority proposes to interconnect with the West Lawrence Water Authority and Hartselle Utilities water systems. West Lawrence currently purchases water from the City of Moulton. Hartselle currently purchases water from Decatur Utilities and provides water exclusively to the Town of Falkville. Both sources have been tested and revealed to have emerging contaminants within the drinking water. WM/EL operates a reverse osmosis system which eliminates emerging contaminants from drinking water. Interconnection to WM/EL would provide the communities with increased water quality and allow the systems to comply with the proposed MCL standard for PFOA and PFAS contaminants.

Project Description:
PFAS Remediation Pilot Study and Preliminary
Design
Attachment Project Name:
PFAS Remediation Pilot Study and Preliminary
Design
Attachment Project Description: The Saraland Water and Sewer Service (SWSS) proposes to conduct a pilot study and perform preliminary engineering services to identify the appropriate PFAS remediation technology for its drinking water system. The results of the pilot study will be used to determine the basis of design. The preliminary engineering design will provide conceptual plans and cost estimates for the installation of PFAS remediation system(s).

Applicant Name:
Albertville, Municipal Utilities Board of the City of,
Water System
Project Description:
Albertville WTP Improvements for PFAS
mitigation
Attachment Project Description: The Albertville Municipal Utilities Board proposes to perform pilot testing on treatment of PFAS throughout the Water Treatment system. The proposed project would ensure the correct method is chosen in order to meet and/or exceed the proposed MCL limit (4 ppt).

Attachment Project Name: Sheffield Water Treatment Plant PFAS Treatment
Attachment Project Description: The Project Scope includes water treatment plant improvements needed to mitigate PFAS. The project Scope includes treatment evaluation and pilot testing. The proposed project would improve water quality and remove PFAS to levels that meet and/or exceed the proposed MCL (4 ppt).

The Coosa Valley Water Supply District (CVWSD) is a regional water supply created by a joint partnership between the Odenville Utilities Board, the City of Pell City, the City of Springville and St. Clair County. The proposed project will evaluate treatment processes (pilot studies and testing) of PFAS throughout the system. The proposed project would ensure the correct method is chosen in order to meet and/or exceed the proposed MCL limit (4 ppt).

Applicant Name: Opelika, Utilities Board of the City of
Attachment Project Description: Opelika Water Board proposes to treat PFAS in the finished water from the Betts WTP by using granular activated carbon filter treatment. The proposed project will include site preparation, lab, and piping improvements for incorporation of carbon treatment into the water treatment process and will contribute to meeting and/or exceeding the proposed MCL limit (4 ppt).

Attachment Project Name:
Deep Water Production Well, Treatment Building, &
Transmission Main
Attachment Project Description: The Loachapoka Water Authority proposes to develop a replacement deep water production well which will not be contaminated with PFAS. The proposed funding will assist in the development of these new facilities and result in water supply levels of PFAS that will meet or exceed the proposed MCL (4.0 ppt)

Attachment Project Name:
Replacement of Contaminated Well with a New
Well
Attachment Project Description: Grand Bay Water proposes to develop a new water supply well that does not contain PFAS exceeding the proposed MCL limit (4.0 ppt). This source of funding will assist the system in constructing test wells and constructing a new well that will not contain PFAS above the proposed MCL (4.0 ppt) and the goal of 0.0 ppt.
